title: I Died of a Heart Attack For 20 Minutes & What I Heard Changed EVERYTHING!
description:
Kevin Mohatt was a 60-year-old fifth degree black belt playing ice hockey on Valentine's Day when he felt like he suddenly weighed 800 pounds. He managed to drive home, stopped at a red light - and died. What followed was over 20 minutes of CPR, multiple shocks, a hole drilled into his bone, and a medical team that had already written him off. His 18-year-old daughter was the only one who refused to let go.

In this interview, Kevin shares what he experienced while he was gone - floating above his own body on a light pole, describing the exact fire trucks, ambulance numbers and police cars at the scene with impossible accuracy, and then being engulfed in an indescribable wall of pure light where he heard his late mother's voice say "not yet, not yet." He also opens up about the brutal road that followed - losing everything, crawling up stairs, and the moment a phone call saved his life just as he was ready to give up.
